title: FPV Dronie
creator: Ruben Caser
award_or_showcase_provenance: Official winner, Dronie category, 6th Annual New York City Drone Film Festival (NYCDFF) -- corroborated this session via AirVuz's own video page (airvuz.com/video/Ruben-Caser----Dronie-----NYCDFF) and AirVuz's '6th Annual New York City Drone Film Festival - Dronie Category' collection page, plus an AirVuz FPV Facebook post captioned 'NYCDFF Dronie Winner | Congratulations Ruben Caser! "Dronie by FPV" is the official winner of the Dronie category at the New York City Drone Film Festival!'
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (single-take 'dronie' / short-form novelty format -- a second, independent Dronie-category exemplar alongside acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json and chrisxgxc-the-last-dronie.json already in this pack) -- built to the same rigor/honesty standard as bbc-earth-aerial.json's original worked example. `source_url`/`title`/`creator` were independently verified this session via YouTube's own oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pxe-8H2TxVY&format=json), which returned title='FPV Dronie', author_name='Ruben Caser', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@RubenCaser', provider_name='YouTube' -- an exact field-for-field match to this candidate's title/creator, so this is a direct primary-source confirmation, not a restatement of the discovery-stage claim. A WebFetch of the watch page itself (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Pxe-8H2TxVY) was also attempted to check for a Creative Commons marking, description text, upload date, and duration, but returned only YouTube's generic truncated footer/navigation/legal boilerplate -- no video metadata, license widget, or description was extractable from that fetch in this session; this is disclosed rather than papered over, matching this pack's existing precedent for JS-rendered YouTube watch pages (e.g. flaviufpv-madeira-fanal-forest-fpv.json). The award claim (Dronie category winner, 6th Annual NYCDFF) was NOT found on the festival's own nycdronefilmfestival.com/winners page in this session -- that page's visible content only lists Dronie-category winners through 2019 (WASHED UP by Acopian & Guilfoyle, THE LAST DRONIE by Chris Castor, FAMILY ISLAND by Florian Fischer, THE SHARK DRONE by Behind The Mask, FLOATING by Florian Fischer), consistent with the 6th Annual (2020) results being hosted off-site; the 6th Annual's results are instead directly hosted on AirVuz, NYCDFF's stated media partner for that year. A WebFetch of AirVuz's own dedicated video page for this entry (https://www.airvuz.com/video/Ruben-Caser----Dronie-----NYCDFF?id=5eaf5e479b9b00765370f247) returned the title '"Dronie" by FPV', creator 'Ruben Caser', an explicit statement that this video 'won the Dronie category at the 6th Annual NYCDFF', a staff description calling it 'an extremely creative take on the dronie shot', and a filming location of Nimmons Bridge, Newtown, Victoria, Australia -- an independent primary-source confirmation of both the win and the creator match. A WebSearch this session additionally surfaced AirVuz's '6th Annual New York City Drone Film Festival - Dronie Category' collection page and an AirVuz FPV Facebook post explicitly captioned 'NYCDFF Dronie Winner | Congratulations Ruben Caser! "Dronie by FPV" is the official winner of the Dronie category at the New York City Drone Film Festival!' -- two further independent corroborating sources for the same award claim, none of which conflict with each other on creator name or award category. One nuance worth flagging honestly: the discovery-stage description supplied for this task characterized the shot as 'ending on the pilot' at the bridge, but AirVuz's own staff description instead states the continuous shot ends with the drone being caught by another person waiting at the bridge (not necessarily the pilot themself stepping into frame) -- this session could not resolve that specific detail further without live playback, so it is noted as an unverified nuance rather than silently adopted or silently dropped; it does not affect the verified title/creator/award/platform fields this record actually relies on. `platform` is 'youtube', confirmed both by the oEmbed provider_name field and the source_url's own host; this is not an Instagram URL, so the Instagram media-extraction prohibition is not triggered, though `local_media_path` is still left null unconditionally per this project's blanket no-download policy regardless of platform. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking was found in either the oEmbed response (which carries no license field) or the truncated watch-page fetch, per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not carry a duration field, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session; rather than fabricate a plausible-looking runtime, this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint, and no live playback of this specific video was performed in this session. Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.73 and exposure=0.73 sit slightly above acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json's 0.68/0.66 (that clip was shot on a first-generation 2016 DJI Mavic Pro in flat overcast Icelandic light, a genuinely lower technical baseline) and roughly level with chrisxgxc-the-last-dronie.json's 0.72/0.73 (another verified Dronie-category award winner) -- there is no confirmed drone model, resolution, or export setting disclosed anywhere in the oEmbed response or the truncated watch-page fetch for this specific clip, so these sit at a modest mid-pack level reflecting festival-juried recognition without a positive hardware/reach signal to lift them further. motion_smoothness=0.77 is set above sharpness/exposure, following this pack's established convention (see acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json's identical reasoning) of crediting motion quality when a clip's entire creative premise depends on it: a 'dronie' is definitionally judged on the smoothness and precision of one continuous, uncut drone move, and AirVuz's own staff description explicitly calls out 'the continuous nature of the shot' as the video's defining, award-winning quality -- a genuine, disclosed textual signal, not an arbitrary guess, though still qualitative rather than a frame-level motion measurement. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-estimated: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.73, exposure=0.73, motion_smoothness=0.77, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.7433333333333333 (rounded to 0.7433 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: estimated_cut_count=0 follows directly from text-provenance evidence, not from having watched the clip: a 'dronie' is definitionally a single continuous, uncut drone-selfie take, and AirVuz's own staff description of this specific entry explicitly emphasizes 'the continuous nature of the shot' as its central, award-recognized creative device. avg_shot_length_seconds is left null despite estimated_cut_count=0 because, unlike acopian-guilfoyle-washed-up-reynisfjara-dronie.json (where Vimeo's oEmbed directly returned a numeric duration=15), no duration field was available anywhere in this session's YouTube oEmbed response or the truncated watch-page fetch for this specific video -- so setting avg_shot_length_seconds to the full runtime would require a runtime figure this session never actually confirmed; it stays unset rather than estimated. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session, so transition_styles_observed stays empty and review_method is 'text_provenance_only'.
